content: p The US military mistakenly shot down a Customs and Border Protection  CBP  drone near the Mexican border in a strike that reportedly used a laser-based anti-drone system. The CBP  a href    drones  a  to track people crossing the border.  p   p  Congressional aides told Reuters the Pentagon used the high-energy laser system to shoot down a Customs and Border Protection drone near the Mexican border, in an area that often has incursions from Mexican drones used by drug cartels,  Reuters  a href    last night.  p   p The FAA closed some airspace along the border with Mexico in Fort Hancock, Texas, on Thursday with a  a href    announcing  a  temporary flight restrictions for special security reasons. The restrictions are in place until June 24 but could be lifted earlier. There are conflicting reports on which day the strike happened, with The New York Times  a href    that the strike occurred Thursday and  a href    writing that the Federal Aviation Administration  FAA   was notified Wednesday after the event occurred.   p  p  a href    full article  a   p   p  a href

metadata.author: Jon Brodkin
